description:
You are a machine-learning engineer setting up an automatic data-preparation routine.  
    For now the routine itself already exists; all you must do is provide the cron
    schedule file that DevOps will later install with the system cron daemon.

    Goal  
    • Create a single-line cron schedule file at  
      /home/user/ml_training/cron/daily_prep.cron  

    What that line must look like  
    • Run every day at 01:30 (24-hour clock).  
    • Execute the script /home/user/ml_training/bin/prepare_data.sh  
    • Append both stdout and stderr from the script to  
      /home/user/ml_training/logs/prepare_data.log  
    • Format: five time-fields, one space, full script path, one space,
      redirection string >>, one space, full log path, one space, 2>&1  
      (do not include extra spaces).  
    • The file must contain exactly that one line followed by a single trailing
      newline character (ASCII 0x0A).

    Directory requirements  
    1. /home/user/ml_training/cron/         – must exist after you finish.  
    2. /home/user/ml_training/bin/          – must exist (the script is already there).  
    3. /home/user/ml_training/logs/         – must exist (log file may or may not yet exist).

    Verification  
    Automated grading will perform the following checks:

    a) /home/user/ml_training/cron/daily_prep.cron exists.  
    b) The file has **exactly** one non-blank line and ends with a single newline.  
    c) The line is textually identical to  
       30 1 * * * /home/user/ml_training/bin/prepare_data.sh >> /home/user/ml_training/logs/prepare_data.log 2>&1  
       (case-sensitive, no leading or trailing spaces).  
    d) The three directories listed above exist.

    You only need to issue one or two shell commands: create the directories (if they
    are not already present) and create/write the cron file with the correct single
    line.  No need to modify the actual system crontab or run sudo.

    Remember: the grading script checks the text of the file and directory
    existence; it does NOT execute the cron job.
truth:
Expected final state for verification:

    Directories that must exist:
      /home/user/ml_training/
      /home/user/ml_training/cron/
      /home/user/ml_training/bin/
      /home/user/ml_training/logs/

    File that must exist:
      Path: /home/user/ml_training/cron/daily_prep.cron
      Exact contents (including final newline):
30 1 * * * /home/user/ml_training/bin/prepare_data.sh >> /home/user/ml_training/logs/prepare_data.log 2>&1\n
      (Where “\n” represents a single newline character, ASCII 0x0A.)

    No other files are required for the purposes of this task.
